medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The 2 weeks before, she was treated with a total dose of 30 U onabotulinumtoxina (Botox) in the right axillary for hyperhidrosis. She did not report any neurological or systemic disorders.
medical_history.family_history: Family history of neuromuscular disorders also was negative.
medical_history.social_history: She did not smoke, consume alcohol, or have any history of drug or substance abuse.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: A noticeable weakness was present in her bulbar and upper limb muscles. The pupils, and the extraocular, neck, trunk, and lower limb muscles were clinically spared. Sensory examination and deep tendon reflexes were normal.

other_tests.findings: Normal sensory, diffusely low compound muscle action potential (CMAP) amplitudes with normal latencies and velocities, and normal late responses
Slow RNS showed a decremental response in proximal muscles, and postexercise CMAP evaluation showed incremental responses between 50% and 160%

Denervating potentials (fibrillation potentials, positive sharp waves [PSW]) were seen, and motor unit action potentials (MUAP) were unstable, short, small, and polyphasic, similar to those of myopathic pattern. Recruitment of motor units was relatively normal

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with nasal speech and progressive dysphagia after receiving a therapeutic dose of onabotulinumtoxinA (Botox) for hyperhidrosis. Clinical examination revealed weakness in bulbar and upper limb muscles. Electrodiagnostic findings, including repetitive nerve stimulation (RNS) and needle electromyography (EMG), were consistent with a neuromuscular junction disorder compatible with subacute botulism. The patient's symptoms and diagnostic tests supported the diagnosis of iatrogenic botulism.
